description:
Karaite agreement regarding cemetery usage and the authority of communal officials over burial practices. Location: presumably Cairo, based on the paleography and surnames mentioned. Dated Sunday 16 Nisan 5438 AM (1678 CE). Several communal "ḥeverīm ve-gabbayīm" listed in l. 2 agree to conditions regarding travel to and usage of the communal cemetery—in all cases, pending the authorization of the Rosh Mishmarah Avraham b. Aharon Rofe and an unnamed gabbay. One of the conditions mentions the pallbearers praying at a synagogue (אלי בית הכנסת תב׳ ות׳ ירוחו יצלו ענדה) and making payments to shamāshīm for different types of burial, perhaps denoting internal sections of the cemetery: וגעלו ללשמשאין פי אלקבר אלגדיד ארבעה פצה ופי אלעתיק תלאתה פצה ופי אלפסקיה נצפין פצה. The penalty for breaking certain conditions is payment of a raṭl or half-raṭl of (candle?) wax. The communal agreement is followed by an exceptional number of signatures, most of which appear to be autographs rather than scribal copies on behalf of signees.

Given that much of the early modern Karaite community resided in Cairo and made trips southward toward Fusṭāt/Old Cairo in order to bury the deceased at al-Bassatīn, it is possible that the synagogue referenced here is the medieval-era Karaite synagogue on Maṣaṣa Street of Fusṭāt. The local chronicler Yosef Sambari notes that the sacred site was in ruins by the 1670s (Sambari, Sefer Divrei Yosef, ed. Shimon Shtober, 235-236). For another seventeenth-century document dealing with Karaite burial practices, see: Yevr.-Arab. II 1406 (PGPID 27947). MCD.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בהספרייה הלאומית רוסיה.
